mechanism_narrative: Non-farm payrolls growth reflects aggregate income growth which drives corporate revenues; sustained growth in PAYEMS predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following months of positive PAYEMS growth are not positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration of headline inflation beyond the Fed's implicit target causes market participants to expect faster monetary tightening, which raises real discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 2.5% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: When triggered, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and retail sales signals a broad-based demand expansion that amplifies earnings surprises, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month only when both series are jointly rising.
failure_condition: Instances where both INDPRO and RSAFS are rising do not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or the joint signal's information coefficient is lower than the best single-series signal.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already below the natural rate, further declines in UNRATE signal an overheating labor market that triggers aggressive Fed tightening, reversing the typical positive correlation and producing negative equity returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with unemployment below natural rate
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of months, or the mean return is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth captures capacity utilization and corporate operating leverage; positive momentum in INDPRO predicts positive near-term S&P 500 returns through earnings expansion.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum does not coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 52% of weeks.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below a critical threshold signal a collapsing residential investment sector that drags broad GDP growth and consumer wealth effects, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When HOUST 3-month average is below 1200, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the nominal federal funds rate is below 2% and unemployment is below 5%, an accommodative monetary stance in a tight labor market expands corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint conditions of FEDFUNDS below 2% and UNRATE below 5% do not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend signals persistent aggregate demand that supports top-line revenue growth across sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter annualized GDPC1 growth above 2% does not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of quarters.
